twitter_thread: 28 out of 50 software stocks I reviewed claim AI-powered features, but only 6 show actual AI-driven revenue
Amdocs and ServiceNow stand out with 15% and 20% of their revenue coming from AI-integrated products
ServiceNow's AI-powered workflow automation has cut customer onboarding time by 30 days
Integrating AI into software stocks can cost $750k to $1.2m, with 60% of projects taking over 12 months to complete
Focus on AI applications with clear ROI, like automating customer support or predicting sales trends
When evaluating AI-driven software stocks, look for companies with 10%+ of revenue from AI-driven products
What's your experience with AI-driven software stocks, and which ones do you think have a competitive edge? #aiinvesting #softwarestocks
pack.linkedin: I reviewed 50 software stocks and found that only 6 have actual AI-driven revenue. Amdocs and ServiceNow are two examples of companies that have successfully integrated AI into their core products, with 15% and 20% of their revenue coming from AI-integrated products. ServiceNow's AI-powered workflow automation has cut customer onboarding time by 30 days. However, integrating AI can be costly and time-consuming, with estimated expenses of $750k to $1.2m and 60% of projects taking over 12 months to complete. What's your take on AI-driven software stocks, and which companies do you think have a real competitive edge?
tiktok_hooks: I dug into 50 software stocks and found the AI hype is mostly just that
The real cost of integrating AI into software stocks will shock you
Which software stock will crack the code on AI-driven growth?
pack.reddit_headline: Are 90% of AI-driven software stocks just pretending to be AI-powered?
